reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (headless Chrome, real download+run, light+dark, mobile+desktop) — revision 1

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — real transformers.js@3.7.5 run in a Web Worker: fresh profile → Download (~26 MB, no auto-download) → auto-init ready → Classify on the cats sample returned tiger cat 96.2%, tabby 1.3%, sock 0.6% (correct ImageNet labels), 176 ms WASM, 1000 classes. Pre-verified independently too: resnet-50 q8 via pipeline() on the same image → tiger cat 0.96, confirming the low-level processor→model path matches. screenshots: /home/paulkinlan/tmp/w18bshots/resnet-classification-{light,dark,mobile}.png
rubric.notes: DISTINCT architecture family from the built ViT/ConvNeXt (same task): ResNet-50 is the ORIGINAL deep-residual CNN (He et al., 2015) — identity skip connections, bottleneck blocks. New architecture FAMILY, not a new task.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — output.logits is [1,1000]; the worker computes softmax + top-k + entropy + top-1 margin. Semantic sanity holds: the cats photo yields cat classes ranked top (tiger cat 96.2%), the See-inside table shows the raw logit next to the softmax %, and the backbone lineage race reports unanimous/majority/split verdicts over ResNet+ConvNeXt+ViT top-1s. conformance: io-shape-semantic=manual (agent verdict recorded).
rubric.notes: 1000 classes (no background class), same label space as ViT and ConvNeXt — which is what makes the three-way race/vote directly comparable.

rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): declares-model-id=pass (Xenova/resnet-50 linked to card), declares-task=pass (image-classification), declares-backend=pass (WebAssembly), declares-quantisation=pass (q8), inference-off-main-thread=pass (new Worker via the shared classify-ui.js ClassifierEngine). At-a-glance also states params (~25.6M), bottleneck-block structure, stage layout (3/4/6/3), Apache-2.0.
rubric.notes: q8 verified NON-degenerate for ResNet-50 — unlike MobileNetV2's depthwise-separable convs, ResNet's plain 1×1/3×3 convs quantise cleanly and return correct labels, so q8 (not fp32) is the honest choice.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — fresh profile shows 'Download model (~26 MB)' and does NOT auto-download; clicking streams progress and the shared loader auto-inits to 'Model ready'. Cached reload (new tab, same profile) → state=ready with NO Download button (only 'Clear cached model'). conformance: shared-auto-init-loader=pass, auto-init-on-cached=manual.
rubric.notes: Verified across the state matrix: first visit (absent→Download) + current-cached (auto-init, no button).

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — streamed download progress; Retry surfaced by the shared loader on failure; classification errors go to a .status.err region ('Classification failed: …'); the lineage race loads ConvNeXt (~29 MB) and ViT (~88 MB) lazily with their own honest progress lines. Runs on the WASM path with no GPU (headless no-GPU produced real labels). conformance: live-status-region=pass, errors-on-the-page=pass; honest-progress-retry-offline + runs-without-webgpu-fallback = manual.

Agent verdict — Classify button, top-k slider (live label), keyboard-operable dropzone + sample thumbnails (Enter/Space), 'Race on this image' three-way button, the residual-block/skip-connection <details>, and per-sub-page controls (batch tag slider + tag-cloud filter chips; the wild transform toggle chips + rotate slider; the three-way ensemble button) all operable. conformance: controls-operable=manual.

Agent verdict — every label comes from the on-device worker run (verified real output tiger cat 96.2% / tabby 1.3%). The lineage race and the three-way vote reuse the same real ClassifierEngine driving the ResNet, ConvNeXt and ViT workers; no canned result anywhere. conformance: no-fake-output=manual.

conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. role=status aria-live loader + run status; probability bars are role=meter with aria-valuenow/aria-label; sample thumbnails keyboard-operable; transform toggles set aria-pressed; chips have min-block-size 40px.
rubric.notes: responsive-check flagged sub-24px targets (range-slider tracks / thumbnail height); agent-reviewed as acceptable — the actionable chips/buttons meet 40px and the sliders are native controls with adequate touch slop.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — desktop 1280×800 (light) + dark and mobile 360×740 DPR3 all render with no horizontal overflow (mobile measured 0px); the at-a-glance table and the three-column versus-grid wrap (grid repeat(auto-fit, minmax(240px,1fr)) so the ResNet/ConvNeXt/ViT columns stack 3→1 on narrow screens); bar labels use overflow-wrap:anywhere. responsive-check programmatic pass on both classes; screenshots reviewed. conformance: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass, parity-both-classes=manual.

Agent verdict — all inference (processor + model forward) runs in a Web Worker via the shared ClassifierEngine; the control UI (sliders, toggles) never blocks on inference; the lineage race runs ResNet + ConvNeXt + ViT with Promise.all off-thread; the batch-tagging loop awaits each off-thread classify so it never blocks the main thread. Main-thread eval stayed responsive during load; 0 console errors. conformance: inp-stays-low=manual.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and multi-column comparison layout without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Reach for intrinsic sizing + flexible tracks (fr, minmax()) before fixed widths; use repeat(auto-fit, minmax()) for responsive card grids; min-inline-size:0 / overflow-wrap on items with long unbreakable content.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the three-backbone race + ensemble panels use .versus-grid = grid repeat(auto-fit, minmax(240px,1fr)) so ResNet/ConvNeXt/ViT sit side-by-side on desktop and stack 3→1 on mobile with no overflow; versus-col has min-inline-size:0; bar labels use overflow-wrap:anywhere; the run panel uses flex:1 1 280px with flex-wrap.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; mobile screenshot measured 0px horizontal overflow

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live status region loading progress and keyboard-operable controls
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label every control; announce state in a role=status aria-live region; adequate tap targets; keyboard operability for custom controls.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + run status are role=status aria-live=polite; probability bars are role=meter with aria-valuenow; sample thumbnails and dropzone handle Enter/Space; transform toggles expose aria-pressed; chips have min-block-size 40px.

guidanceConsulted.query: batch process many images without blocking the main thread / keep INP low
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Yield to the main thread during long loops so input + rendering stay responsive; move heavy work off-thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inference runs in a Web Worker (ClassifierEngine), so the batch-tagging loop awaits an off-thread classify per image and the main thread is never blocked; the race runs all three workers concurrently with Promise.all.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0 console errors; UI responsive during the 176 ms WASM run and the batch loop

guidanceConsulted.query: keep inference off the main thread in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy/background work off the main thread so the UI stays responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all classification runs in a Web Worker; the lineage race/three-way vote run the ResNet, ConvNeXt and ViT workers concurrently off-thread, keeping the control UI responsive.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0 console errors; main-thread eval stayed responsive during load

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page nav.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in the shared public/styles.css; the page links it.

openQuestions: Would a WebGPU execution-provider path (honest WASM fallback retained) make the three-way lineage race meaningfully snappier on capable devices, given ViT is the ~88 MB bottleneck?
Is q8 ResNet-50 measurably less accurate than fp32 on hard/adversarial images, and worth surfacing next to the newer backbones in the race?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a transfer-learning demo: freeze ResNet-50's penultimate features in-browser and train a tiny linear head on a handful of user-provided example images (few-shot custom classifier), showing ResNet's classic role as a feature extractor.
Add a shared 'backbone lineage' timeline surface that the ResNet, ConvNeXt and ViT pages all link to, plotting accuracy/latency/params across the three eras.

summary: Revision-1 agent build+matrix pass for resnet-classification: a NEW image-classification demo for a DISTINCT architecture FAMILY (the original 2015 deep-residual CNN) on the same ImageNet task as the built ViT and ConvNeXt. Real ~26 MB download + on-device transformers.js q8 run verified in headless Chrome (cats sample → tiger cat 96.2%, tabby 1.3%, 176 ms WASM, 1000 classes); q8 confirmed non-degenerate. The ResNet-vs-ConvNeXt-vs-ViT backbone lineage race loads the two newer models lazily and reports a unanimous/majority/split verdict plus per-model latency, making the 2015→2022 architecture evolution legible. Cached reload auto-inits with no Download button; all 4 sub-pages (basics / practical batch-tagging / wild texture-vs-shape / multimodel three-way vote) auto-init clean. Light+dark and mobile+desktop screenshots reviewed (no overflow, legible, ≥40px controls). 23 immutable conformance assertions authored; both gates green.
